headline: Google might not kneecap the Pixel 11a with an old processor
summary: The Pixel 11a is rumored to feature a flagship-grade Tensor G6 processor. This would be a welcome change after the Pixel 10a shipped with an older chip. Fans are hopeful the 'a' series will return to modern chipsets.

headline: Google is open-sourcing its 3D emoji
summary: Google has made its extensive library of 3D emoji designs open-source. Developers and creators can now use these emoji in their own projects. The company shared insights into their 3D design process on World Emoji Day.

headline: GoPro's discounted Max 2 bundle includes $100 worth of accessories
summary: The GoPro Max 2 accessory bundle is currently discounted to $369. This special offer includes $100 worth of extra accessories. It's a notable deal for the high-end 360-degree action camera.

headline: The Guardian's Carter Sherman fondly remembers being terrified by Ocarina of Time
summary: Journalist Carter Sherman reflects on her childhood fear of 'The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time.' Sherman is an award-winning reporter known for her work on sex and gender issues. The article likely explores her personal connection to the classic game.

headline: Christopher Nolan's Long and Winding Road to The Odyssey
summary: Christopher Nolan's latest epic, 'The Odyssey,' reflects his nearly three-decade career. The film is described as drawing on his extensive filmmaking journey. It’s seen as a culmination of his unique directorial style and themes.

headline: Disco Elysium Dev ZA/UM Announces Layoffs Affecting Up to 32 Staff
summary: ZA/UM, the studio behind 'Disco Elysium,' has announced significant layoffs. Up to 32 staff members are being impacted by the job cuts. This news comes just two months after the release of their latest title, 'Zero Parades: For Dead Spies.'

headline: Christopher Nolan's Take on the Gods of The Odyssey Is the Key to Unlocking the Film's Biggest Mystery
summary: Nolan's 'The Odyssey' utilizes gods, like Zendaya's Athena, to set up the film's core mystery. The concept of 'Zeus' Law' is highlighted as central to understanding the plot. The article delves into the director's unique interpretation of mythological figures.

headline: Christopher Nolan Says It Will Be 'At Least' 3 Years Before We See His Next Film, After The Odyssey Pushed Him to His Limits
summary: Christopher Nolan stated his next film won't be released for at least three years. Directing 'The Odyssey' was an extremely demanding experience for him. The filmmaker needs a significant break after his latest ambitious project.

headline: Burnham to announce plans for new North Sea oil and gas drilling
summary: Andy Burnham is set to announce new plans for North Sea oil and gas drilling. This move creates controversy given Labour's 2024 manifesto pledge. The manifesto previously promised not to issue new drilling licenses.

headline: Ruthless plotter? Flip-flopper? Dad dancer? Insiders reveal the real Andy Burnham
summary: Laura Kuenssberg explores the true character of Andy Burnham. The report features insights from over 20 people who know him well. It aims to uncover the authentic personality behind the public figure.

headline: Burnham is 'people pleaser' with 'airy fairy' plans, says Kemi Badenoch
summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch criticized Andy Burnham as a 'people pleaser.' She accused him of having 'airy fairy' plans and misunderstanding national priorities. The remarks were made during an interview with Laura Kuenssberg.

headline: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
summary: Two US troops were killed and one is missing following an Iranian attack in Jordan. Jordan reported intercepting 10 Iranian missiles fired overnight. This incident marks a significant escalation in regional tensions.
